programme.title: The Agenda
programme.desc: A weekly talk-show looking beyond the headlines and “accepted wisdom” to find the real story. Hosted by award-winning journalist and presenter Stephen Cole, The Agenda quite simply sets The Agenda . You won’t find the same-old talking heads recycling platitudes; instead thought-provoking guests to debate and explore key issues of today’s world. Using virtual reality graphics to bring subjects to life, Stephen will navigate through subjects ranging from “Prove That Climate Change Exists” to “Who Will Win the Race to Space”. Opposing views will be given opportunity to flourish and develop into a meaningful conversation. The Agenda: No nonsense talk; Challenging perceptions.

programme.title: The Agenda
programme.desc: A weekly talk-show looking beyond the headlines and “accepted wisdom” to find the real story. Hosted by award-winning journalist and presenter Stephen Cole, The Agenda quite simply sets The Agenda . You won’t find the same-old talking heads recycling platitudes; instead thought-provoking guests to debate and explore key issues of today’s world. Using virtual reality graphics to bring subjects to life, Stephen will navigate through subjects ranging from “Prove That Climate Change Exists” to “Who Will Win the Race to Space”. Opposing views will be given opportunity to flourish and develop into a meaningful conversation. The Agenda: No nonsense talk; Challenging perceptions.

programme.title: Razor
programme.desc: CGTN's brand new science and technology show, covering cutting edge developments from all over the world. RAZOR will show you the world's most powerful laser and the world's smallest anti-cancer treatment. It will take you from the weird world of quantum computing to the boundaries of the cosmos. An international team of reporters will follow the guidance of Occam's Razor - the principle that the simplest explanation is the best one. RAZOR will show that we are already ""living in the future"!

programme.title: Best Eats
programme.desc: Food connects people! A feast for the eyes that spotlights the cuisines of China in a way that’s bound to set the mouths of the whole globe watering!

programme.title: Youthful China
programme.desc: The youth of a country is the future of that country. A series of documentaries focusing on the younger generation of China in which you get to see what they are achieving and what makes them tick!

programme.title: Pixel Collage
programme.desc: A well-crafted collage of selected short documentaries telling stories about all aspects of China! People, technology, culture, and wildlife, all are showcased in Internet-friendly form.

programme.title: Today’s Top Pick
programme.desc: Documentaries in this slot are rebroadcasts of our most-viewed and most popular stories of the season. They feature important social, economic, environmental or other policy-driven issues, affecting lives nationwide or simply in one small village. A broad selection of top productions by Chinese top documentary producers.

programme.title: Humanities and Geography
programme.desc: A strand devoted to the nature and wildlife of China, the people who lived alongside them, and the great and diverse landscapes with which they all contend.

programme.title: Living China
programme.desc: Real life stories told from the personal perspective. Moving, dramatic, sorrows and joys, expressed emphatically and expressed quietly. Watch closely to see and understand the real China as it is lived.

programme.title: Special Edition
programme.desc: Prestige Chinese documentary production, tackling serious subjects with the best cinematography, editing, and all round production values with a unique Chinese perspective.

programme.title: Journeys and Discoveries
programme.desc: A documentary series showcasing the history of China and its people.

programme.title: History in the Frame
programme.desc: A documentary series featuring historical and archaeological exploration.

programme.title: DOCUMENTAL
programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.title: ASÍ ES CHINA
programme.desc: Descubre para el televidente el gran legado cultural e histórico de las distintas regiones y etnias de China. También le acerca a la vida y las costumbres de la gente común y le muestra la evolución del país en aspectos como educación, sociedad y protección del medio ambiente y del patrimonio histórico y natural.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: RONDA ARTÍSTICA
programme.desc: Se exhibe la diversidad de cantos y danzas, óperas locales tradicionales, música y artes folklóricas de China.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: AMÉRICA AHORA
programme.desc: Es un programa nuevo del canal, de corte noticioso con estilo de revista, y que se transmite una vez a la semana. Aquí ofrecemos reportajes y análisis en profundidad sobre los temas más debatidos y de mayor actualidad en la zona americana, y en especial en América Latina.

programme.title: DÍALOGO
programme.desc: Convoca a celebridades, eruditos y funcionarios gubernamentales tanto de China como del exterior, para debatir temas de palpitante actualidad e interés general y de proyección internacional.

programme.title: EL PROYECTOR
programme.desc: Acérquese a la vida de la China antigua y moderna a través de las producciones dramáticas de CGTN-Español. La combinación perfecta de historia y ficción chinas en la pantalla de su televisor.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: SABOREANDO CHINA
programme.desc: Aprenda a preparar las recetas más populares de China. Nuestro chef le revelará de forma práctica los secretos del arte culinario de esta nación asiática. Conozca los ingredientes más utilizados en la variada cocina regional, los orígenes de los platos e incluso sus beneficios para la salud. En De China a tu cocina, el espectador podrá experimentar a la vez la riqueza tradicional y la variedad gastronómica de China.

programme.title: NIHAO CHINA
programme.desc: Es la clase de chino de CGTN-Español. Las series “Aprendamos juntos pinyin”, “Comunícate en chino” y “Viajando y aprendiendo chino”, abordan diferentes niveles de aprendizaje de la nueva lengua de moda en el mundo, brindando así a los televidentes la posibilidad de ampliar su vocabulario, mejorar su gramática y dominar las frases y expresiones más usuales del idioma con más hablantes en el planeta.

programme.title: DOCUMENTAL
programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.title: ASÍ ES CHINA
programme.desc: Descubre para el televidente el gran legado cultural e histórico de las distintas regiones y etnias de China. También le acerca a la vida y las costumbres de la gente común y le muestra la evolución del país en aspectos como educación, sociedad y protección del medio ambiente y del patrimonio histórico y natural.

programme.title: DOCUMENTAL
programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.title: ASÍ ES CHINA
programme.desc: Descubre para el televidente el gran legado cultural e histórico de las distintas regiones y etnias de China. También le acerca a la vida y las costumbres de la gente común y le muestra la evolución del país en aspectos como educación, sociedad y protección del medio ambiente y del patrimonio histórico y natural.

programme.title: PRISMA
programme.desc: Prisma es un programa cultural producido por CGTN en español en colaboración con el multimedio de comunicación latinoamericano teleSUR. Su objetivo es mostrar a los televidentes la riqueza cultural de China y el mundo hispanohablante, elevar el conocimiento de la cultura china en los países de habla hispana y establecer una plataforma de diálogo entre ambas partes. Se trata de un programa mensual de 30 minutos de duración que comenzó a emitirse en agosto de 2016. Está compuesto de tres segmentos que destacan por su diversidad temática. Primero, un espacio de noticias culturales; después, una semblanza de alguna personalidad del ámbito cultural latinoamericano; y finalmente un documental sobre aspectos de la cultura china.

programme.title: RONDA ARTÍSTICA
programme.desc: Se exhibe la diversidad de cantos y danzas, óperas locales tradicionales, música y artes folklóricas de China.

programme.desc: Acérquese a la vida de la China antigua y moderna a través de las producciones dramáticas de CGTN-Español. La combinación perfecta de historia y ficción chinas en la pantalla de su televisor.

programme.title: SABOREANDO CHINA
programme.desc: Aprenda a preparar las recetas más populares de China. Nuestro chef le revelará de forma práctica los secretos del arte culinario de esta nación asiática. Conozca los ingredientes más utilizados en la variada cocina regional, los orígenes de los platos e incluso sus beneficios para la salud. En De China a tu cocina, el espectador podrá experimentar a la vez la riqueza tradicional y la variedad gastronómica de China.

programme.title: NIHAO CHINA
programme.desc: Es la clase de chino de CGTN-Español. Las series “Aprendamos juntos pinyin”, “Comunícate en chino” y “Viajando y aprendiendo chino”, abordan diferentes niveles de aprendizaje de la nueva lengua de moda en el mundo, brindando así a los televidentes la posibilidad de ampliar su vocabulario, mejorar su gramática y dominar las frases y expresiones más usuales del idioma con más hablantes en el planeta.

programme.title: DOCUMENTAL
programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.title: ASÍ ES CHINA
programme.desc: Descubre para el televidente el gran legado cultural e histórico de las distintas regiones y etnias de China. También le acerca a la vida y las costumbres de la gente común y le muestra la evolución del país en aspectos como educación, sociedad y protección del medio ambiente y del patrimonio histórico y natural.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: PRISMA
programme.desc: Prisma es un programa cultural producido por CGTN en español en colaboración con el multimedio de comunicación latinoamericano teleSUR. Su objetivo es mostrar a los televidentes la riqueza cultural de China y el mundo hispanohablante, elevar el conocimiento de la cultura china en los países de habla hispana y establecer una plataforma de diálogo entre ambas partes. Se trata de un programa mensual de 30 minutos de duración que comenzó a emitirse en agosto de 2016. Está compuesto de tres segmentos que destacan por su diversidad temática. Primero, un espacio de noticias culturales; después, una semblanza de alguna personalidad del ámbito cultural latinoamericano; y finalmente un documental sobre aspectos de la cultura china.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: HORA INFANTIL
programme.desc: Una selección de las mejores series chinas de dibujos animados, dobladas al español. Hora Infantil es una ventana a través de la cual los niños y adolescentes de los países de habla hispana pueden llegar a comprender mejor qué es y cómo es China.

programme.desc: Descubre para el televidente el gran legado cultural e histórico de las distintas regiones y etnias de China. También le acerca a la vida y las costumbres de la gente común y le muestra la evolución del país en aspectos como educación, sociedad y protección del medio ambiente y del patrimonio histórico y natural.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: SABOREANDO CHINA
programme.desc: Aprenda a preparar las recetas más populares de China. Nuestro chef le revelará de forma práctica los secretos del arte culinario de esta nación asiática. Conozca los ingredientes más utilizados en la variada cocina regional, los orígenes de los platos e incluso sus beneficios para la salud. En De China a tu cocina, el espectador podrá experimentar a la vez la riqueza tradicional y la variedad gastronómica de China.

programme.title: NIHAO CHINA
programme.desc: Es la clase de chino de CGTN-Español. Las series “Aprendamos juntos pinyin”, “Comunícate en chino” y “Viajando y aprendiendo chino”, abordan diferentes niveles de aprendizaje de la nueva lengua de moda en el mundo, brindando así a los televidentes la posibilidad de ampliar su vocabulario, mejorar su gramática y dominar las frases y expresiones más usuales del idioma con más hablantes en el planeta.

programme.title: RONDA ARTÍSTICA
programme.desc: Se exhibe la diversidad de cantos y danzas, óperas locales tradicionales, música y artes folklóricas de China.

programme.title: DIARIOS DE VIAJE
programme.desc: Es un espacio que muestra al televidente la gran diversidad existente en China a través de entretenidos y cautivadores viajes alrededor del país. En Diarios de Viaje nuestros televidentes encontrarán hermosos paisajes y destinos de incalculable valor histórico, turístico y cultural. Modernidad y tradición, el presente y el pasado se unen en un solo programa.

programme.title: DÍALOGO
programme.desc: Convoca a celebridades, eruditos y funcionarios gubernamentales tanto de China como del exterior, para debatir temas de palpitante actualidad e interés general y de proyección internacional.

programme.title: DOCUMENTAL
programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.title: EL PROYECTOR
programme.desc: Acérquese a la vida de la China antigua y moderna a través de las producciones dramáticas de CGTN-Español. La combinación perfecta de historia y ficción chinas en la pantalla de su televisor.

programme.title: SABOREANDO CHINA
programme.desc: Aprenda a preparar las recetas más populares de China. Nuestro chef le revelará de forma práctica los secretos del arte culinario de esta nación asiática. programme.title: China Capital Market H1: Review and Outlook
programme.desc: International investors' confidence towards China continues to rise with foreign capital steadily flowing into its equity and bond markets. BizTalk will speak to guests from Morgan Stanley and Eurasia Group on Tuesday, June 30th – the last trading day of the first half of the year – to analyze the reason behind accelerating allocation of Chinese stocks and bonds by foreign financial institutions.

programme.title: Global Business-American Edition Ep.2
programme.desc: Global Business is the flagship business show of CGTN English Channel, bringing you the latest economic data, business trends and policies.

programme.title: Global Business-Asian Edition Ep.2
programme.desc: Global Business is the flagship business show of CGTN English Channel, bringing you the latest economic data, business trends and policies.

programme.title: Meet China: From Little Toys to Global Factories, How Chinese Creativity Travels
programme.desc: In this episode of Meet China, we explore how “Made in China” is taking on new forms. A monster toy becomes a global collectible, an ocean park in southern China turns wonder into repeat visits, and a smart EV factory in Zhejiang shows how cars are built for overseas markets. In central China’s Xuchang, a small city supplies much of the world’s hair products, while in Guangxi, an Italian chef blends pizza with traditional medicinal food. From blind boxes to electric vehicles, wigs and a yam-and-lamb pizza, this episode reveals how Chinese creativity, scale and culture are reaching the world in unexpected ways.

programme.title: BizTalk | Summer Davos special: New opportunities for growth in China
programme.desc:
The 17th Annual Meeting of the New Champions, also known as Summer Davos, is underway in Dalian. During the forum, China’s technological innovation, green development and business environment have once again become key topics of discussion.
In this special episode of BizTalk, CGTN speaks with Liu Zhenmin, China’s special envoy for climate change, Joe Ngai, chairman of McKinsey Greater China, and Leo Lee, president of Novartis China, to explore how China is cultivating new growth drivers amid a new wave of industrial transformation, and why “the next China is still China”.

programme.title: Biztalk Special Coverage on AI EP3
programme.desc: The Token Economy Demand for AI tokens is surging, with trends like “tokenmaxxing” accelerating the race for computing power. As pressure mounts on computing and energy infrastructure, China is leveraging its clean energy strengths to support AI growth, while “token exports” emerge as a new way to deliver AI services to global users. What’s the future like for the “token economy”? Join us on BizTalk as we explore the economics behind the AI revolution.

programme.title: The World Cup Economy
programme.desc: As the FIFA World Cup kicks off, the spotlight is not only on the field, but also on the massive economic opportunities unfolding beyond the stadiums. From tourism to business partnerships, the world's top sporting event is bringing people, markets and ideas closer together. But what does it take to turn global exposure into long-term growth? And what are the new trends shaping sports marketing? Join us on BizTalk as we explore the business opportunities behind "the beautiful game."

programme.title: China Agenda 2026
programme.desc: Special coverage of China's annual sessions of its top legislature and political advisory body, known as the "Two Sessions".

programme.title: China's concert boom: Why Gen Z is filling the stadiums
programme.desc:
From packed stadium shows to sold-out performances in smaller cities, China's live music scene is expanding rapidly. Young audiences are turning concerts into a cultural routine: traveling across cities, sharing the experience online and building collective memories around live performances.
The boom now extends far beyond the stage, boosting tourism, retail spending and local economies. In this episode of Unboxing China, we explore how concerts are reshaping consumption patterns and becoming a new driver of cultural and urban development.

programme.title: China's rural CEO experiment: How a Dai village is reinventing itself
programme.desc:
During the Dai New Year, CGTN reporter Lincoln Humphries visited Manluanzhan, a traditional Dai village near China's southwestern border in Yunnan Province.
There, "sleeping" assets are being awakened: an abandoned water tower has been turned into a café, and traditional Dai houses have been expanded to offer new types of homestays.
At the center of this experiment is an unusual figure — a rural CEO who helps balance economic development with the Dai community's traditions as the village charts its own path forward.
In this episode of #BizFocus, Lincoln Humphries shows how a village in Xishuangbanna brings fresh vitality and authentic traditions together.

programme.title: The new keeper of the ancient village in the deep mountains
programme.desc: Deep in the mountains of Fujian, Yanxiang village is home to centuries-old Hakka architecture and an aging population. But a post 1995-born returnee is giving the ancient village a new future. Follow CGTN’s He Jingyi to see how a young village chief blends modern ideas with traditional heritage, breathing new life into this millennium-old rural settlement. #BizFocus

programme.title: How rafting helped one village find new life
programme.desc: In Sankengtan Village, Guangdong Province, a river that once flowed quietly through everyday life is now part of the Gulong Gorge rafting course—a global destination drawing millions of visitors each year. What was once a quiet rural community has been transformed by tourism, as local residents build new livelihoods around the river and the opportunities it brings. In this episode of BizFocus, CGTN's Vivienne Nunis joins those braving the white water to find out more.

programme.desc: The Token Economy Demand for AI tokens is surging, with trends like “tokenmaxxing” accelerating the race for computing power. As pressure mounts on computing and energy infrastructure, China is leveraging its clean energy strengths to support AI growth, while “token exports” emerge as a new way to deliver AI services to global users. What’s the future like for the “token economy”? Join us on BizTalk as we explore the economics behind the AI revolution.

programme.title: China's rural CEO experiment: How a Dai village is reinventing itself
programme.desc:
During the Dai New Year, CGTN reporter Lincoln Humphries visited Manluanzhan, a traditional Dai village near China's southwestern border in Yunnan Province.
There, "sleeping" assets are being awakened: an abandoned water tower has been turned into a café, and traditional Dai houses have been expanded to offer new types of homestays.
At the center of this experiment is an unusual figure — a rural CEO who helps balance economic development with the Dai community's traditions as the village charts its own path forward.
In this episode of #BizFocus, Lincoln Humphries shows how a village in Xishuangbanna brings fresh vitality and authentic traditions together.

programme.title: The new keeper of the ancient village in the deep mountains
programme.desc: Deep in the mountains of Fujian, Yanxiang village is home to centuries-old Hakka architecture and an aging population. But a post 1995-born returnee is giving the ancient village a new future. Follow CGTN’s He Jingyi to see how a young village chief blends modern ideas with traditional heritage, breathing new life into this millennium-old rural settlement. #BizFocus

programme.title: How rafting helped one village find new life
programme.desc: In Sankengtan Village, Guangdong Province, a river that once flowed quietly through everyday life is now part of the Gulong Gorge rafting course—a global destination drawing millions of visitors each year. What was once a quiet rural community has been transformed by tourism, as local residents build new livelihoods around the river and the opportunities it brings. In this episode of BizFocus, CGTN's Vivienne Nunis joins those braving the white water to find out more.

programme.title: China Agenda 2026

programme.title: China's concert boom: Why Gen Z is filling the stadiums
programme.desc:
From packed stadium shows to sold-out performances in smaller cities, China's live music scene is expanding rapidly. Young audiences are turning concerts into a cultural routine: traveling across cities, sharing the experience online and building collective memories around live performances.
The boom now extends far beyond the stage, boosting tourism, retail spending and local economies. In this episode of Unboxing China, we explore how concerts are reshaping consumption patterns and becoming a new driver of cultural and urban development.

BizTalk will speak to guests from Morgan Stanley and Eurasia Group on Tuesday, June 30th – the last trading day of the first half of the year – to analyze the reason behind accelerating allocation of Chinese stocks and bonds by foreign financial institutions.

programme.title: China Capital Market H1: Review and Outlook
programme.desc: International investors' confidence towards China continues to rise with foreign capital steadily flowing into its equity and bond markets. BizTalk will speak to guests from Morgan Stanley and Eurasia Group on Tuesday, June 30th – the last trading day of the first half of the year – to analyze the reason behind accelerating allocation of Chinese stocks and bonds by foreign financial institutions.

programme.title: Global Business-American Edition Ep.2
programme.desc: Global Business is the flagship business show of CGTN English Channel, bringing you the latest economic data, business trends and policies.

programme.title: Global Business-Asian Edition Ep.2
programme.desc: Global Business is the flagship business show of CGTN English Channel, bringing you the latest economic data, business trends and policies.

programme.title: Meet China: From Little Toys to Global Factories, How Chinese Creativity Travels
programme.desc: In this episode of Meet China, we explore how “Made in China” is taking on new forms. A monster toy becomes a global collectible, an ocean park in southern China turns wonder into repeat visits, and a smart EV factory in Zhejiang shows how cars are built for overseas markets. In central China’s Xuchang, a small city supplies much of the world’s hair products, while in Guangxi, an Italian chef blends pizza with traditional medicinal food. From blind boxes to electric vehicles, wigs and a yam-and-lamb pizza, this episode reveals how Chinese creativity, scale and culture are reaching the world in unexpected ways.

programme.title: BizTalk | Summer Davos special: New opportunities for growth in China
programme.desc:
The 17th Annual Meeting of the New Champions, also known as Summer Davos, is underway in Dalian. During the forum, China’s technological innovation, green development and business environment have once again become key topics of discussion.
In this special episode of BizTalk, CGTN speaks with Liu Zhenmin, China’s special envoy for climate change, Joe Ngai, chairman of McKinsey Greater China, and Leo Lee, president of Novartis China, to explore how China is cultivating new growth drivers amid a new wave of industrial transformation, and why “the next China is still China”.

programme.title: Biztalk Special Coverage on AI EP3
programme.desc: The Token Economy Demand for AI tokens is surging, with trends like “tokenmaxxing” accelerating the race for computing power. As pressure mounts on computing and energy infrastructure, China is leveraging its clean energy strengths to support AI growth, while “token exports” emerge as a new way to deliver AI services to global users. What’s the future like for the “token economy”? Join us on BizTalk as we explore the economics behind the AI revolution.

programme.title: China's rural CEO experiment: How a Dai village is reinventing itself
programme.desc:
During the Dai New Year, CGTN reporter Lincoln Humphries visited Manluanzhan, a traditional Dai village near China's southwestern border in Yunnan Province.
There, "sleeping" assets are being awakened: an abandoned water tower has been turned into a café, and traditional Dai houses have been expanded to offer new types of homestays.
At the center of this experiment is an unusual figure — a rural CEO who helps balance economic development with the Dai community's traditions as the village charts its own path forward.
In this episode of #BizFocus, Lincoln Humphries shows how a village in Xishuangbanna brings fresh vitality and authentic traditions together.

programme.title: The new keeper of the ancient village in the deep mountains
programme.desc: Deep in the mountains of Fujian, Yanxiang village is home to centuries-old Hakka architecture and an aging population. But a post 1995-born returnee is giving the ancient village a new future. Follow CGTN’s He Jingyi to see how a young village chief blends modern ideas with traditional heritage, breathing new life into this millennium-old rural settlement. #BizFocus

programme.title: How rafting helped one village find new life
programme.desc: In Sankengtan Village, Guangdong Province, a river that once flowed quietly through everyday life is now part of the Gulong Gorge rafting course—a global destination drawing millions of visitors each year. What was once a quiet rural community has been transformed by tourism, as local residents build new livelihoods around the river and the opportunities it brings. In this episode of BizFocus, CGTN's Vivienne Nunis joins those braving the white water to find out more.

programme.title: Global Business-Asian Edition Ep.1
programme.desc: Global Business is the flagship business show of CGTN English Channel, bringing you the latest economic data, business trends and policies.

programme.title: Enchanting Xiamen Ep.2: New Jewel
programme.desc: In the second episode of our Xiamen series, host Tianran He and co-host Laura, an American who has lived in Xiamen for 7 years, guide you to discover Xiamen's internationalization, modernization, and unique fashion vibe. First, they cycle along the island road, taking in the stunning coastal scenery of the famous Round-the-Island Road, and step into Zengcuoan — once a small fishing village, now a thriving creative hub — to showcase its remarkable transformation. Next, Laura leads Tianran to experience the daily life of expats in Xiamen: exploring the lively coffee bar street, and joining a fun gathering at a stylish French restaurant. A passionate lover of Chinese culture, Laura invites Tianran to experience a traditional Chinese tea ceremony. After that, the pair head to Xiamen’s fashion central business district, experimenting with bold new looks in clothing stores that have caught the eye of the global fashion community. Finally, Tianran visits an egret habitat to meet photographers who document these beautiful birds, and together they admire the grace of Xiamen's iconic egrets.

programme.title: Shunde's flavors: where tradition blends with vivid daily life
programme.desc: Shunde, a gastronomic gem in the Pearl River Delta, neighboring Hong Kong, uses masterful craft and sincere heart to transform local treasures – from crispy fish skin to fried milk – into culinary wonders. Now, two friends, Julian Waghann from the United States and Absalom Absalom from Namibia, are setting out to taste the flavors and feel the warmth of Shunde's countryside with the locals.

programme.title: Foodwise: Fuding
programme.desc: The hosts are in Fuding, Fujian Province in southeast China for the Dragon Boat Festival, where teams of rowers compete against each other in narrow dragon-shaped boats. From making and eating zongzi rice dumplings to trying local street food, the hosts take part in the festivities before exploring the world of white tea, from visiting its “mother tree” to drinking it in a zen temple, and even feasting on a white tea banquet!

programme.title: Foodwise: Wuyi Mountains
programme.desc: The hosts are in the Wuyi Mountains of Fujian Province, the birthplace of oolong tea. Here they enjoy tea ceremony on a bamboo raft, feast on everything from smoked goose to lotus seed soup, and explore an ancient town that once sold tea to Russia. They discover that here it’s all about terroir, and that nestled within the cliffs of the Wuyi Mountains is the most expensive tea in the world.

programme.title: Bird's-eye China Ep.4
programme.desc: Shaanxi, almost right in the middle of China, is a true land of contrasts. South of the Qinling Mountains that divide the province, the terrain has all the allure of south China, while north of this great dividing range lies the Guanzhong Plain and the austere and otherworldly Loess Plateau. Join us for a journey into a province that presents two equally beautiful and fascinating faces to the world!

programme.title: Bird's-eye China Ep.4
programme.desc: Shaanxi, almost right in the middle of China, is a true land of contrasts. South of the Qinling Mountains that divide the province, the terrain has all the allure of south China, while north of this great dividing range lies the Guanzhong Plain and the austere and otherworldly Loess Plateau. Join us for a journey into a province that presents two equally beautiful and fascinating faces to the world!

programme.title: As Chill as the Pandas in Sichuan
programme.desc: Why are pandas so chill? CGTN's Ioana heads to Sichuan to find out, embarking on a journey that's as bashi (effortlessly satisfying) as it is unforgettable. The documentary "As Chill as the Pandas in Sichuan" invites you to slow down, tune in, and discover what it truly means to live a bashi life.

programme.title: Shunde's flavors: where tradition blends with vivid daily life
programme.desc: Shunde, a gastronomic gem in the Pearl River Delta, neighboring Hong Kong, uses masterful craft and sincere heart to transform local treasures – from crispy fish skin to fried milk – into culinary wonders. Now, two friends, Julian Waghann from the United States and Absalom Absalom from Namibia, are setting out to taste the flavors and feel the warmth of Shunde's countryside with the locals.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Five Ancestors Fist
programme.desc: In Quanzhou, southeast China, Kleber seeks out masters of the Five Ancestors Fist, a combination of five local kungfu styles. He meets the abbot of a Southern Shaolin Temple, and takes part in the extraordinary training regime used by Shaolin monks to hone their bodies and minds. He experiences the intricacies of Quanzhou’s traditional puppetry, and learns how Five Ancestors Fist incorporated military tactics that allowed its practitioners to band together and fight off pirates.

programme.title: Colors of the Daliang Mountains
programme.desc: In the first episode of our Sichuan Daliang Mountains series, we journey to Liangshan Yi Autonomous Prefecture in Sichuan Province. Setting off from Xichang, the prefectural capital, we first explore Lizhou Ancient Town, the southern gateway of the Southern Silk Road. We then travel to Huili County in the southernmost part of the Daliang Mountains, where we listen to the morning bells and evening drums, try our hand at making green pottery, and pick fresh pomegranates. Finally, we head to the western part of the Greater Liangshan Mountains and step into the mysterious Lugu Lake. We visit a Mosuo family to learn about their unique culture and take part in one of the Mosuo people’s most important rituals: the coming?of?age ceremony.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-4 Medieval Combat
programme.desc: Episode 4 centres on Shanghai, where Eastern and Western martial traditions meet in surprising ways. Kleber visits local practitioners of Historical European Martial Arts (HEMA), trying German and Italian longsword techniques first-hand and seeing how Chinese enthusiasts innovate by combining European swordsmanship with ancient Chinese military manuals. The episode also follows the rise of BUHURT, a European-style armored combat sport now gaining traction in China, featuring locally made traditional Chinese armour in intense full-contact bouts. Through Kleber’s own multicultural martial arts studio and the city’s everyday East-West blend, the episode shows Shanghai as a global hub, a place where classical martial arts find new life and cross-cultural practices thrive.

programme.title: Yangliuqing New Year Painting Art
programme.desc: On May 20, 2006, Yangliuqing New Year Paintings were named as part of China’s national intangible cultural heritage. The New Year painting tradition can be traced back many centuries, to the Ming Dynasty and beyond, reaching the height of its popularity in the Qing Dynasty. There are no restrictions on the themes of the paintings, and there is no blessing they cannot convey. Yangliuqing New Year Paintings are loved all over the country for their beautiful details and vivid colors—but most of all, for the good wishes they impart to anyone who sees them.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.4
programme.desc: In this episode, we arrive in Changsha — the economic and cultural hub of Hunan Province, and a core city in the Yangtze River Middle Reaches Economic Zone. Our lens turns to the "Xiangjiang Ladies" who work in Changsha’s high-tech and equipment manufacturing sectors. Their daily lives and professional endeavors reflect the city’s innovative vitality, while their after-work gatherings also showcase the dazzling night views along both banks of the Xiangjiang River, highlighting Changsha’s distinct charm as a vibrant capital of leisure and entertainment.

Finally, Tianran visits an egret habitat to meet photographers who document these beautiful birds, and together they admire the grace of Xiamen's iconic egrets.

He experiences the intricacies of Quanzhou’s traditional puppetry, and learns how Five Ancestors Fist incorporated military tactics that allowed its practitioners to band together and fight off pirates.

programme.title: Colors of the Daliang Mountains
programme.desc: In the first episode of our Sichuan Daliang Mountains series, we journey to Liangshan Yi Autonomous Prefecture in Sichuan Province. Setting off from Xichang, the prefectural capital, we first explore Lizhou Ancient Town, the southern gateway of the Southern Silk Road. We then travel to Huili County in the southernmost part of the Daliang Mountains, where we listen to the morning bells and evening drums, try our hand at making green pottery, and pick fresh pomegranates. Finally, we head to the western part of the Greater Liangshan Mountains and step into the mysterious Lugu Lake. We visit a Mosuo family to learn about their unique culture and take part in one of the Mosuo people’s most important rituals: the coming?of?age ceremony.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-5 Xingyi Quan
programme.desc: In Episode 5, Kleber travels to Shanxi, the birthplace of Xingyi Quan. He follows Master Cao Bing, a traditional Xingyi Quan teacher and modern combat coach, from Taiyuan’s noodle shops to Pingyao’s ancient streets, and from centuries-old vinegar breweries to Guandi temples, exploring Xingyi Quan’s deep links to Shanxi’s merchant culture and history. Master Cao breaks down the core principles of the “Five Elements and Twelve Forms”, and shows in his modern gym how techniques found in Muay Thai and Jiu-Jitsu can be integrated without losing Xingyi Quan’s philosophical core, guided by the mindset of taking only what is essential. Set against ancient sites such as Yongle Palace, this episode shows how true inner strength is like traditional timber architecture: stable, balanced, and built from within.

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. These vibrant prints, traditionally displayed during Chinese New Year, depict auspicious scenes, deities, and daily life, embodying wishes for prosperity and happiness. Explore the intricate carving and printing techniques that bring these colorful cultural treasures to life.

programme.title: The Pulse
programme.desc: “The Pulse” features musicians performing amidst Guizhou’s stunning natural scenery, bringing to life a harmonious blend of music and landscape.

programme.title: Enchanting Xiamen Ep.3
programme.desc: In the third episode of our Xiamen series, we introduce four expats from BRICS countries and their inspiring lives in Xiamen. Bjorn, from South Africa, is deeply passionate about Chinese food culture and architecture, and is even skilled at oracle bone script calligraphy. Living in Shapowei, Xiamen’s busiest creative district, he knows every street and lane intimately, and has a profound love for the city's charm. Atul Verma, a yoga teacher from India, opened a yoga studio in Xiamen and built a new career here. Surrounded by Xiamen's peaceful Buddhist atmosphere, he has found a true sense of home in this foreign land. Juan Guimaraes, from Brazil, is an international freshman studying medicine at Xiamen University. Filled with curiosity and longing for both the campus and the city, he hopes to live a fulfilling life at the university and fall in love with Xiamen. Rustam Apushev, from Russia, is known to everyone as a loving “super dad”, but his true identity is a former boxing champion with countless championship medals. He met his wife in Xiamen, raised two children, founded his own school, and started a whole new life. “Xiamen is my home,” he says."

programme.title: China-Africa voices:Travel, writing and mutual understanding
programme.desc: A camel, a Samburu guide, and a Chinese traveler walking across Kenya's Rift Valley toward Lake Turkana. It sounds like the beginning of an adventure story. But behind the deserts and survival challenges lies a deeper story about friendship, cultural understanding and China-Africa exchanges.We speak with Chinese photographer Qi Lin, author of the Camel Diaries. Monica Cheru, Chairperson of the Zimbabwe Writers Association, also joins the conversation as we explore how ordinary people are shaping the relationship between China and Africa.

programme.title: China-Africa voices:Travel, writing and mutual understanding
programme.desc: A camel, a Samburu guide, and a Chinese traveler walking across Kenya's Rift Valley toward Lake Turkana. It sounds like the beginning of an adventure story. programme.title: Foodwise: Hangzhou Ep.1
programme.desc: In this episode Tianran and Hannah are in Hangzhou, a city with over 2000 years of history and capital of two ancient Chinese dynasties. From the famous West Lake to delicate Dragon Well tea, our hosts experience Hangzhou's must-do attractions, savouring the local cuisine and trying everything from winter melon shaped like pork belly, to food cooked according to Traditional Chinese Medicine principles, and even Buddhist noodles and Zen tea.

programme.title: Foodwise: Hangzhou Ep.2
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran and Hannah continue their tea-tasting journey along Hangzhou's Qiantang River, exploring the region's historical culture and culinary delights. They head to Anding Mountain, enjoying misty tea at the summit. In Longmen Ancient Town, they dive into traditional Chinese clan culture and savor “Sun Quan seitan”. In the gentrified village of Shishe, Hannah falls in love with “gray soup rice dumplings”, while at Qiandao Lake Tianran tries the best fish head and beluga caviar he’s ever had in his life. Their journey ends with a cup of Jiukeng tea and a visit to ancient tea trees.

programme.title: Bird's-eye China Ep.5
programme.desc: Guarded by mountains in southeast China, Jiangxi channels rivers into Poyang Lake---the Yangtze's vital link. Starting from Mount Lu's misty slopes, watch and marvel as northern plains transition into an undulating land of hills and basins...

programme.desc: Guarded by mountains in southeast China, Jiangxi channels rivers into Poyang Lake---the Yangtze's vital link. Starting from Mount Lu's misty slopes, watch and marvel as northern plains transition into an undulating land of hills and basins...

programme.title: Spring Festival, a Celebration Shared with the World
programme.desc: When people hear "Spring Festival," they often think of fireworks, red lanterns, and lion dances in the streets. But today, these traditions have grown beyond merely a once-a-year celebration, weaving themselves into people's daily lives across China. The documentary "Spring Festival, a Celebration Shared with the World" embarks on a cultural journey across the nation, tracing how ancient Chinese traditions continue to evolve and engage people worldwide.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-6 Shandong Mantis Style
programme.desc: The final episode traces the cross-ocean transmission of Shandong Mantis Style. In the mountains of northern Italy, host Kleber visits Dante Basili, a veteran Italian practitioner in his sixties, who recounts travelling to China 36 years ago to learn Mantis Style and returning home to teach Chinese martial arts ever since. Kleber then heads to Shandong to meet Yu Bo and Yu Tao, sons of Mantis Style grandmaster Yu Hai. He witnesses a traditional apprenticeship ceremony and explores the style’s fighting essence: adopting its spirit rather than merely copying its form. The episode shows how Mantis Style has taken root and flourished in different cultural settings. Ultimately, this journey across Europe and Asia suggests that transmission is not only about techniques, but also about dialogue across time and distance, and a shared resonance of spirit between cultures.

programme.title: Suzhou Fans
programme.desc: Suzhou, known as the “Venice of China,” is also famous for its traditional crafts. And one of the most elegant and exquisite crafts is Suzhou fans. Celebrated for their exquisite design and fine quality, they’re popularly associated with literati. How come Suzhou gave birth to such a variety of fans? How can we appreciate their beauty in the modern world? In this episode, we are discovering more stories behind Suzhou fans.

programme.title: The Pulse
programme.desc: “The Pulse” features musicians performing amidst Guizhou’s stunning natural scenery, bringing to life a harmonious blend of music and landscape.

programme.title: The Pulse
programme.desc: “The Pulse” features musicians performing amidst Guizhou’s stunning natural scenery, bringing to life a harmonious blend of music and landscape.

programme.title: China Bound：Xiamen
programme.desc: What's it like in one of China's happiest cities? Tianran is in sunny Xiamen on China's southeast coast, visiting the city's famous "island of music" and sampling local delicacies like shark meatballs, shacha noodles and fish in "soy sauce water". He'll discover former fishing villages turned hipster havens, drink tea with locals beneath traditional tenement buildings, and visit a student enclave brimming with creative startup energy.

programme.title: Foodwise: Shantou
programme.desc: Tianran and Hannah are in Shantou, Guangdong Province in south China, where they drink more tea than anywhere else in the country. They'll savor the complex aroma of the curiously named "Duck Poo Fragrance" tea, as well as enjoy a more traditional "yum cha" breakfast. They get a taste of traditional handicrafts, from breaking bowls to make inlaid porcelain to rolling "kueh" rice cakes, and feast on everything from sweet and savory street snacks to the finest seafood dining.

programme.title: Foodwise: Shaoguan
programme.desc: Tianran and Hannah are in the picturesque city of Shaoguan in Guangdong Province, South China. They try tea grown on Danxia landforms and feast on mountain produce, before getting hands-on with making rice noodles in a Hakka walled village. They learn about the Hakka people’s southward migration and the effect it had on local tea and food, and end their journey in a thousand-year-old Buddhist temple, seeing how the monks integrate their practice into everyday life, and discovering just why tea is so important to Zen.

programme.title: Aerial China: Beijing
programme.desc: China’s capital of Beijing is a bucket list city for any self-styled world traveler, but let’s be honest, not all of us have the time! Not to worry, because we’re about to embark on an aerial journey over the nation’s heart, giving you a crash course on this massive hub of culture, international exchange, and technological innovation. If you’re afraid of heights, this may be the one time it’s okay to look down!

programme.title: Aerial China: Beijing
programme.desc: China’s capital of Beijing is a bucket list city for any self-styled world traveler, but let’s be honest, not all of us have the time! Not to worry, because we’re about to embark on an aerial journey over the nation’s heart, giving you a crash course on this massive hub of culture, international exchange, and technological innovation. If you’re afraid of heights, this may be the one time it’s okay to look down!

programme.title: Poet of the Sky: The Liuyang Fireworks Art
programme.desc: The documentary "Poet of the Sky: The Liuyang Fireworks Art" follows a heritage master and a visionary show director as they carry a thousand-year-old craft into the future. Originating in Liuyang City, Hunan Province, Liuyang fireworks have evolved from ritual folk traditions into a modern art form that integrates cutting-edge technology. Check out how Liuyang fireworks paint stories across the sky.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-1 Baji Quan
programme.desc: In the first episode of Season 2 of Kung Fu Wise, Baji Quan, host Kleber travels to Mengcun, Cangzhou, in Hebei Province. There, he meets Wu Hao, the young head of the Wu-style Baji Quan school. Together, they journey across the North China Plain, from bustling village markets and the family training hall to the Bohai Sea coast, as Kleber experiences Baji Quan’s explosive power first-hand. Along the way, the episode unpacks the idea of “opening the gate”, the key to focused power, and the spirit of spear work. It goes beyond technique to reveal Cangzhou’s rich martial heritage, tracing how Wu-style Baji Quan has travelled from its hometown to Europe, proof that traditional kung fu can endure, evolve, and thrive today. In the end, Wu Hao answers with action: true inheritance is not only about skills, but about shaping spirit and character.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-6 Shandong Mantis Style
programme.desc: The final episode traces the cross-ocean transmission of Shandong Mantis Style. In the mountains of northern Italy, host Kleber visits Dante Basili, a veteran Italian practitioner in his sixties, who recounts travelling to China 36 years ago to learn Mantis Style and returning home to teach Chinese martial arts ever since. Kleber then heads to Shandong to meet Yu Bo and Yu Tao, sons of Mantis Style grandmaster Yu Hai. Ultimately, this journey across Europe and Asia suggests that transmission is not only about techniques, but also about dialogue across time and distance, and a shared resonance of spirit between cultures.

programme.desc: What's it like in one of China's happiest cities? Tianran is in sunny Xiamen on China's southeast coast, visiting the city's famous "island of music" and sampling local delicacies like shark meatballs, shacha noodles and fish in "soy sauce water". He'll discover former fishing villages turned hipster havens, drink tea with locals beneath traditional tenement buildings, and visit a student enclave brimming with creative startup energy.

programme.title: Foodwise: Shantou
programme.desc: Tianran and Hannah are in Shantou, Guangdong Province in south China, where they drink more tea than anywhere else in the country. They'll savor the complex aroma of the curiously named "Duck Poo Fragrance" tea, as well as enjoy a more traditional "yum cha" breakfast. They get a taste of traditional handicrafts, from breaking bowls to make inlaid porcelain to rolling "kueh" rice cakes, and feast on everything from sweet and savory street snacks to the finest seafood dining.

programme.title: Foodwise: Shaoguan
programme.desc: Tianran and Hannah are in the picturesque city of Shaoguan in Guangdong Province, South China. They try tea grown on Danxia landforms and feast on mountain produce, before getting hands-on with making rice noodles in a Hakka walled village. They learn about the Hakka people’s southward migration and the effect it had on local tea and food, and end their journey in a thousand-year-old Buddhist temple, seeing how the monks integrate their practice into everyday life, and discovering just why tea is so important to Zen.

programme.title: Aerial China: Beijing
programme.desc: China’s capital of Beijing is a bucket list city for any self-styled world traveler, but let’s be honest, not all of us have the time! Not to worry, because we’re about to embark on an aerial journey over the nation’s heart, giving you a crash course on this massive hub of culture, international exchange, and technological innovation. programme.title: Aerial China: Beijing

programme.title: Poet of the Sky: The Liuyang Fireworks Art
programme.desc: The documentary "Poet of the Sky: The Liuyang Fireworks Art" follows a heritage master and a visionary show director as they carry a thousand-year-old craft into the future. Originating in Liuyang City, Hunan Province, Liuyang fireworks have evolved from ritual folk traditions into a modern art form that integrates cutting-edge technology. Check out how Liuyang fireworks paint stories across the sky.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-1 Baji Quan
programme.desc: In the first episode of Season 2 of Kung Fu Wise, Baji Quan, host Kleber travels to Mengcun, Cangzhou, in Hebei Province. There, he meets Wu Hao, the young head of the Wu-style Baji Quan school. Together, they journey across the North China Plain, from bustling village markets and the family training hall to the Bohai Sea coast, as Kleber experiences Baji Quan’s explosive power first-hand. Along the way, the episode unpacks the idea of “opening the gate”, the key to focused power, and the spirit of spear work. It goes beyond technique to reveal Cangzhou’s rich martial heritage, tracing how Wu-style Baji Quan has travelled from its hometown to Europe, proof that traditional kung fu can endure, evolve, and thrive today. In the end, Wu Hao answers with action: true inheritance is not only about skills, but about shaping spirit and character.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-6 Shandong Mantis Style
programme.desc: The final episode traces the cross-ocean transmission of Shandong Mantis Style. In the mountains of northern Italy, host Kleber visits Dante Basili, a veteran Italian practitioner in his sixties, who recounts travelling to China 36 years ago to learn Mantis Style and returning home to teach Chinese martial arts ever since. Kleber then heads to Shandong to meet Yu Bo and Yu Tao, sons of Mantis Style grandmaster Yu Hai. He witnesses a traditional apprenticeship ceremony and explores the style’s fighting essence: adopting its spirit rather than merely copying its form. The episode shows how Mantis Style has taken root and flourished in different cultural settings. Ultimately, this journey across Europe and Asia suggests that transmission is not only about techniques, but also about dialogue across time and distance, and a shared resonance of spirit between cultures.

programme.title: Suzhou Fans
programme.desc: Suzhou, known as the “Venice of China,” is also famous for its traditional crafts. And one of the most elegant and exquisite crafts is Suzhou fans. Celebrated for their exquisite design and fine quality, they’re popularly associated with literati. How come Suzhou gave birth to such a variety of fans? How can we appreciate their beauty in the modern world? In this episode, we are discovering more stories behind Suzhou fans.

programme.title: Poet of the Sky: The Liuyang Fireworks Art
programme.desc: The documentary "Poet of the Sky: The Liuyang Fireworks Art" follows a heritage master and a visionary show director as they carry a thousand-year-old craft into the future. Originating in Liuyang City, Hunan Province, Liuyang fireworks have evolved from ritual folk traditions into a modern art form that integrates cutting-edge technology. Check out how Liuyang fireworks paint stories across the sky.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-1 Baji Quan
programme.desc: In the first episode of Season 2 of Kung Fu Wise, Baji Quan, host Kleber travels to Mengcun, Cangzhou, in Hebei Province. There, he meets Wu Hao, the young head of the Wu-style Baji Quan school. Together, they journey across the North China Plain, from bustling village markets and the family training hall to the Bohai Sea coast, as Kleber experiences Baji Quan’s explosive power first-hand. Along the way, the episode unpacks the idea of “opening the gate”, the key to focused power, and the spirit of spear work. It goes beyond technique to reveal Cangzhou’s rich martial heritage, tracing how Wu-style Baji Quan has travelled from its hometown to Europe, proof that traditional kung fu can endure, evolve, and thrive today. In the end, Wu Hao answers with action: true inheritance is not only about skills, but about shaping spirit and character.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Wing Chun
programme.desc: Martial arts lover Kleber Battaglia is on a mission: To seek out China's kungfu masters, and learn their secrets. From explosive Wing Chun to fluid Taiji Quan, slippery Snake Style to elegant White Crane, he'll learn hidden moves and ancient techniques for defeating powerful opponents. But ?there’s more to kungfu than combat: From culture and philosophy, to wellness and insights from the natural world, Kleber will discover that contained within every kick, strike and throw, is the condensed wisdom of the ancient masters. This is Kungfu-wise. Kleber travels to Foshan, the birthplace of Wing Chun. A practitioner of this style, he seeks out masters of two different lineages: Ip Man and Snake Style. First he visits the Ip Man lineage, based on the legendary kungfu master whose life story spawned hundreds of martial arts movies, with Bruce Lee counting among his students. With Ip Man Wing Chun, Kleber learns how to create powerful short strikes perfectly suited to fighting in Foshan’s narrow alleys. With Snake Style, he experiences unique training methods that give its practitioners superhuman flexibility, allowing them to stick to their opponents, and even fight on boats in canals

programme.title: Chengdu Lacquerware Art
programme.desc: Chengdu lacquerware is unique, thanks to its “three carvings and one engraving” technique. Along with its bold decorative style, this has made it a gem of Chinese lacquer art. Chengdu lacquerware also emphasizes the use of vibrant colors. Pieces are brought to beautiful life by bright red, vivid yellow, emerald green, and other striking hues.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing
programme.desc: From the local delicacies of Beijing's Ox Street to latest hangouts and contemporary cuisine of the Chinese capital, hosts Tianran He and Eva May will give you a true taste of Beijing, and the fascinating stories and people behind them.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing's Hutongs
programme.desc: The Hutongs in Beijing are considered the city's blood vessels, as these traditional alleyways make up the DNA of the capital for its rich history and culture. In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May zigzag their way through the Hutongs and the Forbidden City for a mouth-watering experience full of ancient and modern delicacies. Starring German designer Katrin, Russian artist Liuba and photographer Feng Yu from Beijing, our hosts are taking you along a journey that features fried noodles, dim sum, pancakes and co.!

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing's Hutongs
programme.desc: The Hutongs in Beijing are considered the city's blood vessels, as these traditional alleyways make up the DNA of the capital for its rich history and culture. programme.title: Bird's-eye China : Shanghai
programme.desc: Balancing delicately on an alluvial plain and the Yangtze estuary of China’s east coast, much of Shanghai-literally meaning “on the sea”-sits at an average sea-level elevation of about 4 meters. Bisecting the city is the Huangpu River, dividing it into Puxi (west bank) and Pudong (east bank), where you’ll find its famous international airport. Join us as on an aerial cruise as we journey upstream towards one of the world’s most recognizable skylines.

programme.title: Bird's-eye China : Shanghai
programme.desc: Balancing delicately on an alluvial plain and the Yangtze estuary of China’s east coast, much of Shanghai-literally meaning “on the sea”-sits at an average sea-level elevation of about 4 meters. Bisecting the city is the Huangpu River, dividing it into Puxi (west bank) and Pudong (east bank), where you’ll find its famous international airport. Join us as on an aerial cruise as we journey upstream towards one of the world’s most recognizable skylines.

programme.title: Sculpting the Soul of Tianjin
programme.desc: Let's journey into the soul of Tianjin through the story of Zhang Yu – the sixth-generation inheritor of the famed Clay Figure Zhang family. As part of the documentary series "Next Stop: China," this episode reveals how one man's dedication bridges centuries of artistry with the pulse of a modern metropolis.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-2 Chinese Wrestling
programme.desc: In Episode 2, Chinese Wrestling, host Kleber joins a public training session led by Wang Tongqing, a master preserving the imperial Shanpu Ying wrestling tradition. Swept up in the group’s lively atmosphere, he gets a first-hand feel for the master’s skill. Kleber then invites his Italian friend Paolo to train systematically, uncovering how Chinese wrestling principles connect with other martial arts, from Tai Chi to Wing Chun. Through Master Wang’s story of bringing a formerly imperial martial art into the public sphere, mentoring the next generation, and hoping to take it onto the international stage, the episode shows wrestling as more than a test of strength. It is a cultural bridge between history and the present.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Wing Chun
programme.desc: Martial arts lover Kleber Battaglia is on a mission: To seek out China's kungfu masters, and learn their secrets. From explosive Wing Chun to fluid Taiji Quan, slippery Snake Style to elegant White Crane, he'll learn hidden moves and ancient techniques for defeating powerful opponents. But ?there’s more to kungfu than combat: From culture and philosophy, to wellness and insights from the natural world, Kleber will discover that contained within every kick, strike and throw, is the condensed wisdom of the ancient masters. This is Kungfu-wise. Kleber travels to Foshan, the birthplace of Wing Chun. A practitioner of this style, he seeks out masters of two different lineages: Ip Man and Snake Style. First he visits the Ip Man lineage, based on the legendary kungfu master whose life story spawned hundreds of martial arts movies, with Bruce Lee counting among his students. With Ip Man Wing Chun, Kleber learns how to create powerful short strikes perfectly suited to fighting in Foshan’s narrow alleys. With Snake Style, he experiences unique training methods that give its practitioners superhuman flexibility, allowing them to stick to their opponents, and even fight on boats in canals

programme.title: Sculpting the Soul of Tianjin
programme.desc: Let's journey into the soul of Tianjin through the story of Zhang Yu – the sixth-generation inheritor of the famed Clay Figure Zhang family. As part of the documentary series "Next Stop: China," this episode reveals how one man's dedication bridges centuries of artistry with the pulse of a modern metropolis.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-2 Chinese Wrestling
programme.desc: In Episode 2, Chinese Wrestling, host Kleber joins a public training session led by Wang Tongqing, a master preserving the imperial Shanpu Ying wrestling tradition. Swept up in the group’s lively atmosphere, he gets a first-hand feel for the master’s skill. Kleber then invites his Italian friend Paolo to train systematically, uncovering how Chinese wrestling principles connect with other martial arts, from Tai Chi to Wing Chun. Through Master Wang’s story of bringing a formerly imperial martial art into the public sphere, mentoring the next generation, and hoping to take it onto the international stage, the episode shows wrestling as more than a test of strength. It is a cultural bridge between history and the present.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Wing Chun
programme.desc: Martial arts lover Kleber Battaglia is on a mission: To seek out China's kungfu masters, and learn their secrets. From explosive Wing Chun to fluid Taiji Quan, slippery Snake Style to elegant White Crane, he'll learn hidden moves and ancient techniques for defeating powerful opponents. But ?there’s more to kungfu than combat: From culture and philosophy, to wellness and insights from the natural world, Kleber will discover that contained within every kick, strike and throw, is the condensed wisdom of the ancient masters. This is Kungfu-wise. Kleber travels to Foshan, the birthplace of Wing Chun. A practitioner of this style, he seeks out masters of two different lineages: Ip Man and Snake Style. First he visits the Ip Man lineage, based on the legendary kungfu master whose life story spawned hundreds of martial arts movies, with Bruce Lee counting among his students. With Ip Man Wing Chun, Kleber learns how to create powerful short strikes perfectly suited to fighting in Foshan’s narrow alleys. With Snake Style, he experiences unique training methods that give its practitioners superhuman flexibility, allowing them to stick to their opponents, and even fight on boats in canals

programme.title: Chengdu Lacquerware Art
programme.desc: Chengdu lacquerware is unique, thanks to its “three carvings and one engraving” technique. Along with its bold decorative style, this has made it a gem of Chinese lacquer art. Chengdu lacquerware also emphasizes the use of vibrant colors. Pieces are brought to beautiful life by bright red, vivid yellow, emerald green, and other striking hues.

programme.title: The Pulse
programme.desc: “The Pulse” features musicians performing amidst Guizhou’s stunning natural scenery, bringing to life a harmonious blend of music and landscape.

programme.title: The Pulse
programme.desc: “The Pulse” features musicians performing amidst Guizhou’s stunning natural scenery, bringing to life a harmonious blend of music and landscape.

programme.title: China Bound：Xiamen
programme.desc: What's it like in one of China's happiest cities? Tianran is in sunny Xiamen on China's southeast coast, visiting the city's famous "island of music" and sampling local delicacies like shark meatballs, shacha noodles and fish in "soy sauce water". He'll discover former fishing villages turned hipster havens, drink tea with locals beneath traditional tenement buildings, and visit a student enclave brimming with creative startup energy.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing
programme.desc: From the local delicacies of Beijing's Ox Street to latest hangouts and contemporary cuisine of the Chinese capital, hosts Tianran He and Eva May will give you a true taste of Beijing, and the fascinating stories and people behind them.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing's Hutongs
programme.desc: The Hutongs in Beijing are considered the city's blood vessels, as these traditional alleyways make up the DNA of the capital for its rich history and culture. In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May zigzag their way through the Hutongs and the Forbidden City for a mouth-watering experience full of ancient and modern delicacies. Starring German designer Katrin, Russian artist Liuba and photographer Feng Yu from Beijing, our hosts are taking you along a journey that features fried noodles, dim sum, pancakes and co.!

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing's Hutongs
programme.desc: The Hutongs in Beijing are considered the city's blood vessels, as these traditional alleyways make up the DNA of the capital for its rich history and culture. In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May zigzag their way through the Hutongs and the Forbidden City for a mouth-watering experience full of ancient and modern delicacies. Starring German designer Katrin, Russian artist Liuba and photographer Feng Yu from Beijing, our hosts are taking you along a journey that features fried noodles, dim sum, pancakes and co.!

programme.title: Bird's-eye China : Shanghai
programme.desc: Balancing delicately on an alluvial plain and the Yangtze estuary of China’s east coast, much of Shanghai-literally meaning “on the sea”-sits at an average sea-level elevation of about 4 meters. Bisecting the city is the Huangpu River, dividing it into Puxi (west bank) and Pudong (east bank), where you’ll find its famous international airport. Join us as on an aerial cruise as we journey upstream towards one of the world’s most recognizable skylines.

programme.title: Bird's-eye China : Shanghai
programme.desc: Balancing delicately on an alluvial plain and the Yangtze estuary of China’s east coast, much of Shanghai-literally meaning “on the sea”-sits at an average sea-level elevation of about 4 meters. Bisecting the city is the Huangpu River, dividing it into Puxi (west bank) and Pudong (east bank), where you’ll find its famous international airport. Join us as on an aerial cruise as we journey upstream towards one of the world’s most recognizable skylines.

programme.title: Sculpting the Soul of Tianjin

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-2 Chinese Wrestling
programme.desc: In Episode 2, Chinese Wrestling, host Kleber joins a public training session led by Wang Tongqing, a master preserving the imperial Shanpu Ying wrestling tradition. It is a cultural bridge between history and the present.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber seeks out Liu Weixin, a master of Pak Mei kungfu and on whom the hit game "Sifu" was based. He experiences for himself this style's relentless and lightning-fast strikes, but also learns from its master how to balance life and kungfu, how a master should treat his disciples, and what it means to "eat porridge at night"

programme.title: Jingdezhen Handmade Porcelain
programme.desc: Porcelain has shaped the culture of the whole world. The wisdom behind each of 72 procedures has been refined from generation to generation. The times may change, some techniques may change, but what remains the same is the burning flame of the kiln that keeps setting so many hearts on fire for this ancient craft.

programme.title: China Untapped: Changsha
programme.desc: The city of Changsha is one of central China's powerhouses. It's a leader in both the construction machinery industry and in media – Changsha was China's first UNESCO "Creative City". It's also one of China's best-known cities for its nightlife. The soul of a city is in its people, and this episode focuses on the women of Changsha who are helping to innovate, change and further develop this city they call home.

programme.title: All About the Yak
programme.desc: In this episode, we go to Nangqian, Yushu, Qinghai Province, to get close contact with the Yak in Tibet, which is known as the " Vehicle on the Plateau". During the bustling yak culture festival, people gather from near and far, meadows are crowded, and yak appraisal activities are also in full swing. As the journey continues, we are able to understand several aspects of the yak culture which is closely related to the life of Tibetan people.

programme.title: Foodwise: Changsha
programme.desc: In this episode, our hosts Tianran He and Eva May embark on a journey to Changsha in Hunan Province.?They taste their way through the local cuisine, discover the city's latest art and fashion trends, and find out why this place is one of China's "influencer hotspots", attracting millions of domestic and foreign tourists each year.

programme.title: Foodwise：Qingdao
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May venture to Qingdao, China's beer mecca by the sea. With red tiles and green trees that make up the city's stunning scenery, our hosts embark on another foodie-journey with local delicacies and interesting guest appearances. From drinking beer with a German friend, catching crabs by the ocean and devouring grilled eel the classic Korean way, this episode will leave you hungry for more!

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.1
programme.desc: When Zhejiang's winding river meets the sea it encounters a coastal region studded with islands. On this journey we survey a land of bays, bridges and harbors that is a glorious archipelago of memory, history and culture...

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.1
programme.desc: When Zhejiang's winding river meets the sea it encounters a coastal region studded with islands. On this journey we survey a land of bays, bridges and harbors that is a glorious archipelago of memory, history and culture...

programme.title: A Canopy of Umbrellas, a Sichuan Serenade
programme.desc: The documentary "A Canopy of Umbrellas, a Sichuan Serenade" follows a father and son as they carry forward the centuries-old craft of making Luzhou oil-paper umbrellas. Each umbrella tells a story—not only of skill, but of family and cultural resilience. As part of CGTN’s original series "Next Stop: China," this episode journeys into the lush bamboo forests of Sichuan, where traditional craftsmanship finds new life in the modern world.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-3 Shaolin Style
programme.desc: Episode 3, Shaolin, explores how this thousand-year-old temple has evolved into a unique “kung fu platform” that draws enthusiasts from around the world. Host Kleber immerses himself in Shaolin’s integrated way of life, where Chan (Zen) practice and martial training go hand in hand, and turns the camera toward foreign practitioners pursuing their kung fu dreams both inside and beyond the temple gates. Along the way, he meets international disciples living and training at Shaolin, as well as students learning the rare art of Shaolin Xinyiba under Master Hu Zhengsheng, a former Shaolin monk. At martial arts schools near Songshan Mountain, Kleber also encounters overseas competitors training hard for the arena. Together, their stories reveal Shaolin kung fu’s enduring pull, one that crosses cultures and borders while uniting practitioners through tradition, discipline, and self-mastery.

programme.title: Sanxingdui: Rediscovering a Lost Civilisation
programme.desc: Sanxingdui Site is located at Guanghan, Sichuan Province. It is a site of an ancient city, ancient kingdom and ancient Shu culture in southwest of China, which is the largest in area, longest in time span and richest in cultural connotation. The total area covers 12 square kilometers, which was built over 3000 years ago. The cultural relics at Sanxingdui are precious cultural heritage of mankind, which contain rich historical, cultural and scientific value, and bespeak themselves as the most attractive ones amongst the multitude of the spectacular relics in China. In this episode, under the guide of the presenter, we visit the Sanxingdui Site Garden, archaeological Site and Sanxingdui Museum, show the amazing giant bronze mask, exquisite gold stuff, marvelous bronze divine tree etc., re-explore this mysterious and great civilization 3000 years ago.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei
