programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber meets with two masters of southern Chinese martial arts. He learns from a Hung Ga master that while this kungfu style is often associated with brute force, it’s actually a combination of hard and soft techniques that can also be used in traditional Chinese massage. Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Nanjing Yunjin Brocade: A Traditional Chinese Silk Craft
programme.desc: Nanjing Yunjin Brocade, a traditional Chinese silk handicraft with a history spanning over 1,600 years, derives its name from its radiant and resplendent hues, which resemble clouds in the sky. Renowned for its exquisite materials, meticulous weaving techniques, and vibrant, splendid patterns, Yunjin Brocade is hailed as the "king of brocades." It represents the pinnacle of Chinese silk weaving techniques, encapsulates the essence of Chinese silk craftsmanship, and stands as a brilliant crystallization of China’s silk culture. The core technique of Yunjin Brocade weaving—known as "weaving through the warp and cutting the weft"—is executed manually by two artisans working above and below complex, large-scale looms. Using materials such as silk floss, gold thread, and peacock feather threads, they create luxurious fabrics like imperial dragon robes. To this day, Yunjin Brocade continues to be used in crafting high-end clothing and souvenirs. Creating Yunjin Brocade involves over 120 intricate and sophisticated processes before the final masterpiece takes shape. Having traversed over a millennium, its majestic and luxurious aura remains unchanged. Just like the Qinhuai River, which has flowed through Nanjing for over 3,000 years—clear and steadfast—its gentle currents cut through the annals of history as it journeys toward an even more distant future.

programme.title: China Untapped: Huzhou
programme.desc: Located in the east of China, on the south shore of Taihu Lake, Huzhou has been well-known for its richness in resources and blooming in silk trade since the ancient times. Guided by the concept of "lucid waters and lush mountains are invaluable assets", Huzhou now is committed to developing green industries. In this episode, CGTN hosts Tianran He and Hannah Wilson travelled to Huzhou, where they tried a variety of guesthouse hotels in the Moganshan Mountains; experienced the life of digital nomads in Anji’s countryside; learned about the eco-concepts behind China's traditional architectures in Nanxun Ancient Town; and explored the development of eco-agriculture powered by high-tech along the Taihu Lake. All these phenomena demonstrate that economic development and environmental protection can achieve harmonious coexistence, and that civilization thrives if ecology is prosperous.

programme.title: The Yak and the Nomad
programme.desc: Qinghai’s Yushu Prefecture is one of China’s key yak-producing regions. This trip took us to Nangqian County, where we explored the yak industry that lies at the heart of Tibetan herders’ daily lives. Trekking into the far-flung pastoral corners of the plateau, we listened closely to the stories behind the hurdles and hopes of pastoral life and development.

programme.title: Foodwise: Wuzhen
programme.desc: Join our hosts Tianran He and Eva May as they explore the boisterous event of Wuzhen's International Theatre festival, with colorful features of both theatrical and culinary nature. Foodie Tianran and art-lover Eva are accompanied by ubiquitous local street food, daily carnival performances and meet and greets with fascinating personalities. Let's set the stage for the beautiful merge of food and drama as they taste their way through the charming scene of Wuzhen.

programme.title: Foodwise：Eating Global in Beijing
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May are in search of global cuisines that can be found in Beijing, the 3000-year old capital of China. From honest farm-to-table fare, to the culinary heights of French haute cuisine, and even a cooking class and wine and dine event at a traditional courtyard home in the city center, we'll discover that in Beijing, you can taste the world.

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.2
programme.desc: Sichuan's western mountains are a shield safeguarding a true national treasure: the Chengdu Plain, China's very own land of plenty. Join us as we dive from high plateaus into deep gorges, scale snow peaks, and follow rivers in pursuit of an engineering marvel bequeathed by the ancients...

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.2
programme.desc: Sichuan's western mountains are a shield safeguarding a true national treasure: the Chengdu Plain, China's very own land of plenty. Join us as we dive from high plateaus into deep gorges, scale snow peaks, and follow rivers in pursuit of an engineering marvel bequeathed by the ancients...

programme.title: Shared Roots in Wrestling
programme.desc: The documentary “Shared Roots in Wrestling” follows a young wrestler from Tianjin as he journeys to Inner Mongolia to explore the shared heritage of Chinese-style wrestling and Bökh, the region’s national wrestling tradition. Part of CGTN’s original series “Next Stop: China,” this episode uncovers how strength, skill, and resilience connect athletes across city streets and endless grasslands, carrying forward a legacy that transcends geography and generations.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-4 Medieval Combat
programme.desc: Episode 4 centres on Shanghai, where Eastern and Western martial traditions meet in surprising ways. Kleber visits local practitioners of Historical European Martial Arts (HEMA), trying German and Italian longsword techniques first-hand and seeing how Chinese enthusiasts innovate by combining European swordsmanship with ancient Chinese military manuals. The episode also follows the rise of BUHURT, a European-style armored combat sport now gaining traction in China, featuring locally made traditional Chinese armour in intense full-contact bouts. Through Kleber’s own multicultural martial arts studio and the city’s everyday East-West blend, the episode shows Shanghai as a global hub, a place where classical martial arts find new life and cross-cultural practices thrive.

programme.title: The Yak and the Nomad
programme.desc: Qinghai’s Yushu Prefecture is one of China’s key yak-producing regions. This trip took us to Nangqian County, where we explored the yak industry that lies at the heart of Tibetan herders’ daily lives. Trekking into the far-flung pastoral corners of the plateau, we listened closely to the stories behind the hurdles and hopes of pastoral life and development.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber meets with two masters of southern Chinese martial arts. He learns from a Hung Ga master that while this kungfu style is often associated with brute force, it’s actually a combination of hard and soft techniques that can also be used in traditional Chinese massage. Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Nanjing Yunjin Brocade: A Traditional Chinese Silk Craft
programme.desc: Nanjing Yunjin Brocade, a traditional Chinese silk handicraft with a history spanning over 1,600 years, derives its name from its radiant and resplendent hues, which resemble clouds in the sky. Renowned for its exquisite materials, meticulous weaving techniques, and vibrant, splendid patterns, Yunjin Brocade is hailed as the "king of brocades." It represents the pinnacle of Chinese silk weaving techniques, encapsulates the essence of Chinese silk craftsmanship, and stands as a brilliant crystallization of China’s silk culture. The core technique of Yunjin Brocade weaving—known as "weaving through the warp and cutting the weft"—is executed manually by two artisans working above and below complex, large-scale looms. Using materials such as silk floss, gold thread, and peacock feather threads, they create luxurious fabrics like imperial dragon robes. To this day, Yunjin Brocade continues to be used in crafting high-end clothing and souvenirs. Creating Yunjin Brocade involves over 120 intricate and sophisticated processes before the final masterpiece takes shape. Having traversed over a millennium, its majestic and luxurious aura remains unchanged. Just like the Qinhuai River, which has flowed through Nanjing for over 3,000 years—clear and steadfast—its gentle currents cut through the annals of history as it journeys toward an even more distant future.

programme.title: China Untapped: Huzhou
programme.desc: Located in the east of China, on the south shore of Taihu Lake, Huzhou has been well-known for its richness in resources and blooming in silk trade since the ancient times. Guided by the concept of "lucid waters and lush mountains are invaluable assets", Huzhou now is committed to developing green industries. In this episode, CGTN hosts Tianran He and Hannah Wilson travelled to Huzhou, where they tried a variety of guesthouse hotels in the Moganshan Mountains; experienced the life of digital nomads in Anji’s countryside; learned about the eco-concepts behind China's traditional architectures in Nanxun Ancient Town; and explored the development of eco-agriculture powered by high-tech along the Taihu Lake. All these phenomena demonstrate that economic development and environmental protection can achieve harmonious coexistence, and that civilization thrives if ecology is prosperous.

Trekking into the far-flung pastoral corners of the plateau, we listened closely to the stories behind the hurdles and hopes of pastoral life and development.

The episode also follows the rise of BUHURT, a European-style armored combat sport now gaining traction in China, featuring locally made traditional Chinese armour in intense full-contact bouts. programme.title: The Yak and the Nomad
programme.desc: Qinghai’s Yushu Prefecture is one of China’s key yak-producing regions. This trip took us to Nangqian County, where we explored the yak industry that lies at the heart of Tibetan herders’ daily lives. Trekking into the far-flung pastoral corners of the plateau, we listened closely to the stories behind the hurdles and hopes of pastoral life and development.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of "World Capital of Stone Carving," awarded by the World Crafts Council. The county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been influenced by the area’s close proximity to the sea.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.1
programme.desc: Lhasa, set high on the “Roof of the World” in China’s southwest, is both the capital of Xizang Autonomous Region, and the spiritual heart of Tibetan culture. In this episode, host Tianran He ventures deep into the fabled Potala Palace, discovering ancient meditation caves, priceless relics and golden rooftops. Then, he tries his hand at Tibetan papermaking, learning how a poisonous ingredient is crucial for making paper that can survive for centuries. Finally, Tianran dons his apron and learns to make iconic Tibetan dishes, including one said to reveal your fortune!

programme.title: An Encounter with China’s Endangered Monkeys
programme.desc: In this episode, we head to Baima Snow Mountain Nature Reserve in Diqing Tibetan Autonomous Prefecture, Yunnan Province, to learn about and participate in the conservation work for the rare and precious national treasure: the Yunnan snub-nosed monkey. The presenter ventures into the forest, experiences the daily life of local forest rangers, and witnesses the harmonious relationship between humans and monkeys. We interview "the Monkey Father"—Long Yongcheng, chief scientist of The Nature Conservancy (TNC) and leader of China's Primate Expert Group—who shares stories about the two decades of efforts dedicated to protecting the Yunnan snub-nosed monkey. We also go to the mountains to conduct research alongside young volunteers. Through these activities, we recognize the importance of environmental education for young people in promoting natural ecological balance and harmonious development.

programme.title: Foodwise: Quanzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May are in Quanzhou, the ancient starting point of the Maritime Silk Road. From its covered arcades and Roman columns to its Southeast Asian vibe, our hosts discover that Quanzhou truly is a melting pot of world cultures. Then there’s the food: From the local noodle porridge that utilizes Silk Road spices, to fried vinegar pork and a dessert made from a special algae, there’s plenty of unique dishes to try here. Over a cup of oolong, our hosts will discover how Quanzhou’s tea changed the world, and how trade with Arab civilizations influenced a local fishing village. So join us in Quanzhou, a city that UNESCO designated as an “Emporium of the World”!

programme.title: Foodwise: Xi'an
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May venture to Xi'an, an ancient city with over 3000 years of history, capital to 13 Chinese dynasties. But first, our hosts are going to carb load on street food, from glutinous rice cakes and cakes made out of persimmons, to mutton soup with flatbread and the famous biangbiang noodles. They'll also taste the rich history of Xi'an by experiencing a Qin Dynasty-inspired banquet, and try millennia-old dishes such as trout grilled on a ceramic tile and calabash chicken, a Tang Dynasty delicacy. From the Terracotta Army to the Daming Palace, residence of the Tang emperors, to a dazzling nighttime boulevard that recreates the grandeur of the Tang Dynasty, there's so much to explore in Xi'an. So, get your camera and your stomach ready for CGTN's Foodwise in Xi'an!

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.3
programme.desc: Inner Mongolia spans China from east to west. Little wonder then, that it's such a land of diversity - epic landscapes of forests, grasslands and deserts, Inner Mongolia has it all!

programme.title: Aerial China II-2 Ep.3
programme.desc: Inner Mongolia spans China from east to west. Little wonder then, that it's such a land of diversity - epic landscapes of forests, grasslands and deserts, Inner Mongolia has it all!

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.3
programme.desc: Inner Mongolia spans China from east to west. Little wonder then, that it's such a land of diversity - epic landscapes of forests, grasslands and deserts, Inner Mongolia has it all!

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-5 Xingyi Quan
programme.desc: In Episode 5, Kleber travels to Shanxi, the birthplace of Xingyi Quan. He follows Master Cao Bing, a traditional Xingyi Quan teacher and modern combat coach, from Taiyuan’s noodle shops to Pingyao’s ancient streets, and from centuries-old vinegar breweries to Guandi temples, exploring Xingyi Quan’s deep links to Shanxi’s merchant culture and history. Master Cao breaks down the core principles of the “Five Elements and Twelve Forms”, and shows in his modern gym how techniques found in Muay Thai and Jiu-Jitsu can be integrated without losing Xingyi Quan’s philosophical core, guided by the mindset of taking only what is essential. Set against ancient sites such as Yongle Palace, this episode shows how true inner strength is like traditional timber architecture: stable, balanced, and built from within.

programme.title: All About the Yak
programme.desc: In this episode, we go to Nangqian, Yushu, Qinghai Province, to get close contact with the Yak in Tibet, which is known as the " Vehicle on the Plateau". During the bustling yak culture festival, people gather from near and far, meadows are crowded, and yak appraisal activities are also in full swing. As the journey continues, we are able to understand several aspects of the yak culture which is closely related to the life of Tibetan people.

The presenter ventures into the forest, experiences the daily life of local forest rangers, and witnesses the harmonious relationship between humans and monkeys. We interview "the Monkey Father"—Long Yongcheng, chief scientist of The Nature Conservancy (TNC) and leader of China's Primate Expert Group—who shares stories about the two decades of efforts dedicated to protecting the Yunnan snub-nosed monkey. We also go to the mountains to conduct research alongside young volunteers. Through these activities, we recognize the importance of environmental education for young people in promoting natural ecological balance and harmonious development.

programme.title: The Call of the Plateau
programme.desc: In Qinghai's Nianbaoyuze mountain range, 16 local herders—15 of them women—work tirelessly to protect endangered black-necked cranes and the wetlands they call home, even as the dangers of climate change threaten to undo their efforts.

programme.title: The Call of the Plateau
programme.desc: In Qinghai's Nianbaoyuze mountain range, 16 local herders—15 of them women—work tirelessly to protect endangered black-necked cranes and the wetlands they call home, even as the dangers of climate change threaten to undo their efforts.

programme.title: Xinjiang: Where Horizons Expand Ep.1

programme.title: Timeless Ancient Structures The Temple of Heaven
programme.desc: Built on the southern end of Beijing's Central Axis, the Temple of Heaven has endured over the course of the last 600 years. Also a UNESCO World Heritage Site, it is described as a masterpiece of architecture and landscape design, and is a must-see attraction for domestic and foreign tourists alike. Join us as we explore its fascinating history and the significance of all the wonders it beholds.

programme.title: Zero Hour 3
programme.desc: Europe is feeling the severe impact of climate change--flooding in Germany, droughts in Spain, rising tides in Venice, and wildfires in Siberia. Now, a new generation of scientists is working on solutions. From capturing carbon dioxide directly from the atmosphere to indoor solar cells, the world’s best and brightest are working together, and they’re not stopping until the job is done.

programme.title: Zero Hour 2
programme.desc: Since the late 1970s, the United States has been warming faster than the rest of the world. From heatwaves and coastal flooding in the Northeast to droughts and wildfires in the Southwest, things are looking bleak—but there’s still time. Today, students and scientists are working to mitigate—perhaps even reverse—the worst effects of climate change. The question is: can it be done?

programme.title: Cities of the Future Ep.1
programme.desc: This documentary turns its lens to the essential infrastructure of China’s cities, filming 35 hidden projects—including flood control and drainage, emergency response, and water, electricity, and gas supply. From the perspectives of urban renewal, industrial innovation, and dynamic development, it offers a true record of China's urban growth that values what lies both above and below ground.

From the perspectives of urban renewal, industrial innovation, and dynamic development, it offers a true record of China's urban growth that values what lies both above and below ground.

programme.title: The Call of Glaciers
programme.desc: The Qinghai-Tibet Plateau is one of the world's most climate-sensitive regions. As its indigenous peoples, animals, and plants face the impacts of climate change, a Gen Z environmentalist rediscovers her homeland, with plenty of new insight along the way.

programme.title: Thrill Seeker's Guide to China – Mount Siguniang
programme.desc: Documents the journey of host He Tianran and National Geographic photographer Ma Chunlin through the Changping and Haizi valleys, culminating in their crowning achievement: the summit of Mount Siguniang in Sichuan Province.

programme.title: Dancing with the Plateau
programme.desc: Guided by principles of human-nature harmony, this documentary traces the extraordinary life cycle of the endangered black-necked crane. Comprehensively ?documenting the migration, habitats, and reproduction of these birds, ‘Dancing with the Plateau’ is full of vital scientific insights related to biodiversity conservation and sustainable ecosystem management.

programme.desc: This documentary exposes the heinous crimes of the Japanese Imperial Army's germ warfare in China, restoring historical truth while sending China’s message of "learning from history and cherishing peace."

programme.title: History Speaks 2
programme.desc: During World War II, Japanese militarism continued to expand outward, bringing deep suffering to the nations of East Asia. As time passes, more and more witnesses to history are passing away. Meanwhile, right-wing forces within the Japanese government have attempted to downplay or even cover up this history of aggression, making the tough but necessary task of preserving the memory of the past more urgent than ever.

programme.title: Encounters Along the Silk Road Ep.1
programme.desc: This documentary selects three locations along the Belt and Road with deep historical and cultural roots: Luoyang in Henan, Weinan in Shaanxi, and Dunhuang in Gansu. Through the eyes of Chinese and international youth, it explores past and present Silk Road culture through stories of cultural exchange and mutual learning between China and the world.

programme.title: History Speaks 2
programme.desc: During World War II, Japanese militarism continued to expand outward, bringing deep suffering to the nations of East Asia. As time passes, more and more witnesses to history are passing away. Meanwhile, right-wing forces within the Japanese government have attempted to downplay or even cover up this history of aggression, making the tough but necessary task of preserving the memory of the past more urgent than ever.

programme.title: Encounters Along the Silk Road Ep.1
programme.desc: This documentary selects three locations along the Belt and Road with deep historical and cultural roots: Luoyang in Henan, Weinan in Shaanxi, and Dunhuang in Gansu. Through the eyes of Chinese and international youth, it explores past and present Silk Road culture through stories of cultural exchange and mutual learning between China and the world.

Don't miss this encouraging episode about learning to follow the Word of Faith instead of fear.

En Dios De Milagros, los enfermos son sanados, los cautivos liberados, los oprimidos ministrados, y todos crecen espiritualmente con las enseñanzas y predicaciones de los pastores Hernan y Wendy Castaño.
